title: cerastium fontanum subsp. triviale
intro: Cerastium fontanum subsp. triviale, also known as small mouse-ear chickweed, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Caryophyllaceae family. It is a low-growing, herbaceous perennial that is often found in wet meadows and along streams. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it a popular remedy for skin irritations and wounds. Cerastium fontanum subsp. triviale can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, salves, and infusions, which are said to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and provide relief from skin conditions like eczema and acne.
